subject: Public Health
title: The Future of Addiction Policy
description: A fast breakdown of The Future of Addiction Policy for builders.

hook.text: The world is slowly shifting from criminalizing addiction to treating it—but the transition is uneven and incomplete.

buildup.text: Decriminalization, harm reduction, and treatment expansion are gaining ground—driven by evidence, not ideology.

discovery.text: Digital therapeutics—apps that deliver cognitive behavioral therapy—are expanding access to treatment beyond clinics.

twist.text: New challenges emerge constantly—synthetic opioids, vaping, and online drug markets outpace regulation.

climax.text: A comprehensive approach—prevention, treatment, harm reduction, and social support—offers the best path forward.

punchline.text: Addiction policy is finally catching up with addiction science.

quiz.question: What approach offers the best path forward for addiction?
options: Combining prevention, treatment, harm reduction, and social support
